## [00:00 - 03:01]

Okay, so let's talk about your sales video here. So I'm going to give you a couple of absolutely key rules here that you need to follow. This is if you don't get any, if you only do this, you don't do anything else right, then you're gonna be just fine. And rule number one is that your offer in your sales video, it's all about the transformation. It's not about the stuff. It's about how your prospects life is going to be transformed. It's like, what impact will your product make on them? What change will they have in their life? And I don't care if you're selling golf clubs or you're selling a product about how to play guitar or you're selling a product about how to sell real estate or how to have a massage therapy practice. It doesn't matter. It's still, it's about the transformation. Your product is about the transformation. It's how your prospect, once they buy your product, how is their life gonna change? How is it gonna be better? How are you gonna increase their pleasure or decrease their pain? It's not about whether you deliver it on a bunch of DVDs or it's in a book or it's in a seminar or a webinar or it's a widget or whatever. It's not so much about the stuff. It's about the transformation. The stuff is far, far less important. The actual deliverables, how they get it in their hands isn't nearly as important as what the transformation is. Think about like if you had the cure for cancer, you were selling the cure for cancer, would it matter if it was a pill that they took or if it was a shot that they took or if they had to sit in a machine for a half an hour or whatever? It doesn't matter how it's delivered. Now in the example of like cancer and like if it's radiation treatment and they have to go through this horrible treatment for weeks on end, well then the stuff does matter but for the most part it's the end result that's really important. So you have to focus on that in your sales video. And then rule number two is to refer back to rule number one. In other words, it's all about communicating the transformation. That's what your sales video exists to do. That's the reason for it is to communicate their transformation and then ask for the money. Okay, so a lot of people, I want to get this out of the way right away. A lot of people are really concerned about how long their sales video should be. Now I don't want to be flippant here, but this is important. Don't think, wow, Jeff's got a 27 minute video so I have to make my video 27 minutes long. Or so and so had a nine minute video and I have to make my video nine minutes long. Or so and so had a 50 minute video so I need to make my video 50 minutes long. The deal is your video should be as long as it needs to be to tell the complete story

of your product and to make your offer and then not one second longer. And really this goes for all your videos, whether they're pre-launch videos or sales videos. ## [06:05 - 09:06]

but this is a good starting place. So first of all, you want to open up with a big promise. You want to paint the transformation. You want them to really be able to feel that big promise, that opportunity for their life to change. Then you want to show authority, especially if you can show results. Often in a launch, you're going to, you can basically use that pre-launch content and talk about that pre-launch content, how many views it had, how many comments it had. Maybe you can flash up little screenshots of the pre-launch videos, because just publishing great pre-launch content shows great authority. But basically you want to go opportunity than authority. This is a great place for proof and testimonials. Start to pull in story, whether it's your story or the story of your students. And this is like generally a pretty good chunk of the video in here. You want to foreshadow urgency if you can. So basically everyone would love to put off any buying decision. People just naturally procrastinate on buying decisions. But you want to have scarcity or urgency in your offer. And right here, you can just foreshadow it. Like you can just mention it that this is only a limited time and we're going to get going. We're going to have to start our class soon or something like that. I'll often find some way to foreshadow the urgency early on. Then you basically, here's the deal. You make your offer and you cover your features and your benefits in here. So here often you do talk about your stuff to some degree. But even when you're talking about your stuff, you want to keep on focusing on the transformation. So like if they get five modules and a module one is this, this, and this, well, what do those things actually do for the people? Like they don't care that there's four videos. But here we're going to do four videos that show you how to play the F chord on the guitar. Actually, this is the chord that so many people struggle with. But when you learn that F chord, it unlocks all these different songs, yada, yada, yada. So even when you're talking about what they actually get the stuff, you always want to focus on the transformation. Now in here, you can offer bonuses and then do the revelation of price. Or you can flip those around. You could do the revelation of price first and then the bonuses. But generally, I like to do the bonuses, talk about the bonuses that they get. And then you reveal the price. And when you reveal the price, it's not like, well, I have price justification there. And it's not like you're saying, oh, man, it's $299. And I know that sounds like a lot of money, but it's really not because I give you this, this, and this. That's not the way you do it. You want to talk about price from a position of authority. And the price justification means,

## [09:06 - 12:09]

often I want to do an apples to oranges comparison here, where you're comparing your, let's say, if it's a knowledge-based course, an information course, you can compare it to what a college course cost. What it would cost in real world experience, if you went out and worked for 10 years to get this experience, what would that cost you? So you want to do some price revelation and a justification of price. Then you have to create urgency. This is scarcity. Why should they act now? You always need some type of urgency. Then after urgency is your call to action. This is where you basically tell them, now's the time to buy. You can't do that. You have to tell the complete story of your offer and your product and the transformation before you go through and you make your offer and you reveal the price and all that stuff.
